front: Work: FATE and KEVIN disrupt the FutureMouse conference organized by Marcus Chalfen in this novel
Work: Millat joins the Islamic fundamentalist group KEVIN in this novel
Work: this novel opens with Archie Jones's attempted suicide by gassing himself in his car, interrupted by a butcher
Work: James Wood coined 'hysterical realism' to criticize this novel
Work: Dr. Perret/Dr. Sick, a Nazi scientist who cries blood, is revealed to be alive at the FutureMouse conference
Work: Samad is obsessed with ancestor Mangal Pandey and wants his portrait hung in O'Connell's Pub
Work: Irie cannot identify her baby's father because she had sex with twin brothers twenty minutes apart
Work: Hortense Bowden, a Jehovah's Witness, was born during the Kingston earthquake in this novel
Work: the lesbian shoe-mender known as 'Neena the Niece-of-Shame' appears in this novel
Work: Millat participates in protests against Salman Rushdie over The Satanic Verses
Work: this novel loosely structured after Howards End centers on the rivalry between art historians Howard Belsey and Monty Kipps
Work: a Hector Hippolyte painting of Maitresse Erzulie is a major plot point; left to Kiki in a will
Work: Claire Malcolm teaches Zora at a poetry workshop that visits The Bus Stop in this novel
Work: this novel's title comes from an Elaine Scarry essay about beauty 'and being just'
Work: Keisha changes her name to Natalie and secretly arranges sex with swingers online; Nathan stops her from jumping off a bridge
Work: Felix is stabbed to death for asking a man to move his feet for a pregnant woman on a train
Work: a girl with flat feet takes tap lessons with the dancing prodigy Tracey; the narrator later works for pop diva Aimee
Work: Feel Free includes 'Joy,' 'Generation Why?' on Facebook, and imagines Justin Bieber meeting Martin Buber
work: Essays (Feel Free, Changing My Mind, criticism)

front: Work: 'Two Directions for the Novel' contrasts Joseph O'Neill's Netherland and Tom McCarthy's Remainder
work: Essays (Feel Free, Changing My Mind, criticism)

front: Author: this author's 2023 novel The Fraud fictionalizes the Tichborne case and includes a chapter titled 'Dickens is Dead!'
Author: this author's debut play The Wife of Willesden reimagines Chaucer's Wife of Bath as a Jamaican woman named Alvita
Author: this author wrote The Embassy of Cambodia about the unpaid maid Fatou working for the Derawals
Author: this author's mother is Yvonne Bailey, both featured in the New Daughters of Africa anthology
